description: A modern, visually appealing landing page for DriveEasy Car Rentals — a fictional car rental company. Features a hero section, fleet showcase, how-it-works, testimonials, pricing plans, and a contact/footer section.

features.name: nav_header
features.title: Navigation Header
features.description: Sticky top navigation bar with the DriveEasy logo, nav links (Home, Fleet, How It Works, Pricing, Contact), and a 'Book Now' CTA button. Collapses to a hamburger menu on mobile.
acceptance: Logo and brand name visible
All nav links rendered and anchor-scroll to their sections
Book Now button present with primary styling
Component uses only design tokens — no hardcoded colors/spacing
Accessible: keyboard-navigable, all interactive elements have labels

features.name: hero_section
features.title: Hero Section
features.description: Full-width hero with a bold headline ('Your Journey Starts Here'), a subheadline, a brief value proposition, and two CTAs: 'Browse Fleet' and 'Learn More'. Includes a hero background image placeholder (CSS gradient).
acceptance: Headline, subheadline, and value proposition text rendered
Two CTA buttons rendered with correct primary/secondary styling
Background uses a CSS gradient via design tokens
Visually centered layout on desktop and mobile
Section has id='home' for anchor nav

features.name: fleet_section
features.title: Fleet Showcase
features.description: Grid of car cards, each showing a car name, category (Economy / SUV / Luxury), price per day, key specs (seats, transmission, fuel), and a 'Rent Now' button. Uses made-up data for 6 cars.
acceptance: At least 6 car cards rendered from a static data array
Each card shows: image placeholder, name, category badge, price/day, specs, Rent Now button
Cards use the card design token (background, radius, padding)
Category badges use appropriate color tokens
Section has id='fleet' for anchor nav
Responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3 col desktop)

features.name: how_it_works_section
features.title: How It Works Section
features.description: Three-step process section: 1. Choose Your Car, 2. Book Online, 3. Hit the Road. Each step has an icon (emoji or SVG), a title, and a short description.
acceptance: Three steps rendered with icon, title, description
Visually distinct step numbers or icons
Section has id='how-it-works' for anchor nav
Responsive layout (stack on mobile, row on desktop)

features.name: testimonials_section
features.title: Testimonials Section
features.description: Customer testimonials carousel/grid with 4 made-up reviews. Each review has avatar initials, customer name, rating (stars), and quote text.
acceptance: At least 4 testimonial cards rendered from static data
Each card shows: avatar (initials in a colored circle), name, star rating, quote
Uses card design token for styling
Section has id='testimonials' (can be omitted from nav but must exist in DOM)

features.name: pricing_section
features.title: Pricing Plans Section
features.description: Three pricing tiers: Basic, Standard, Premium. Each tier lists included features, price per day, and a CTA. One tier is visually highlighted as 'Most Popular'.
acceptance: Three pricing cards rendered from static data
Each card: tier name, price/day, feature list, CTA button
Most Popular card visually distinguished (border, badge, or background)
Section has id='pricing' for anchor nav
Responsive layout (stack on mobile, row on desktop)

features.name: contact_footer
features.title: Contact Section and Footer
features.description: Combined contact section with a simple contact form (name, email, message fields + submit button) and a footer with company info, quick links, and copyright.
acceptance: Contact form rendered with name, email, message, submit button
Form fields have accessible labels
Footer has: logo/company name, quick nav links, contact info (made-up address/phone), copyright
Section has id='contact' for anchor nav
Form submission shows a success message (no real API call needed)

features.name: Deploy to Vercel
features.verification: vercel --prod returns a live https:// URL; URL reported to user
